headline: Millions of children are now protected by malaria vaccines
meta_description: Malaria vaccines RTS,S and R21 are rolling out across Africa, protecting millions of children from one of the deadliest diseases.
summary_short: Two safe, effective vaccines are rolling out across Africa, reaching millions of children in the fight against one of humanity's oldest killers.
body:
For most of human history, malaria had no vaccine at all. Now there are two. RTS,S and R21 — both recommended by the World Health Organization — are being rolled out across African countries, and millions of children have already received their doses.

Malaria still takes far too many young lives every year, which is exactly why this matters: large-scale vaccination, together with bed nets and better treatment, is expected to prevent tens of thousands of child deaths annually as programmes expand. A disease that once seemed unbeatable is, dose by dose, losing ground.
